videos.title: WF26: Harness Engineering & Startup Battlefield ft. Garry Tan, Mike Krieger, @t3dotgg , DSPy
videos.summary: Step into the minds of elite tech leaders and startup gurus. As a junior developer, this gives you a front-row seat to the future of software engineering, toolchains like DSPy, and how to build high-impact applications that investors actually care about.

videos.title: [S01:E15] Founder Supercube jana RM20 Juta dalam masa setahun? - Hayfa Amira, Founder Supercube
videos.summary: This interview features Hayfa Amira, the founder of Supercube International, sharing her transition from being a corporate engineer to a full-time entrepreneur. She discusses how her engineering background helped her make data-driven business decisions and scale her artisanal tea brand globally, reaching over RM 20 million in sales during its first year. This video is highly valuable for anyone looking to build a sustainable, structured business with a clear long-term exit strategy.

timestamps.topic: Introducing Hayfa Amira and her journey with Supercube
How her corporate engineering background shaped her business mindset
How Supercube scaled organically to RM 20 million in its first year

videos.title: “Don’t start a company just because you want to IPO” - Pinn Yang, Foodie Media CEO | In The Studio
videos.summary: This interview features the co-founders of Foodie Media, a digital media empire that grew from a side hustle earning less than 1 Ringgit a day to a public company raising 75 million Ringgit. The founders share valuable insights on scaling a business, leveraging personal branding and AI, and navigating the complexities of co-founding a company with a partner.

timestamps.topic: The inspiring origin story of Foodie Media's growth from a side hustle to an IPO
The importance of personal branding and authenticity in the era of AI
How to structure different business pillars (content, creator, commerce, community) for growth

videos.title: How to get tough feedback from someone who cares about you | Chris Wheatley | TEDxSpokane
videos.summary: This engaging TEDx talk explores the transformative power of receiving tough feedback from loved ones and colleagues, revealing how constructive criticism is vital for personal growth. Speaker Chris Wheatley introduces 'TACT,' a practical framework designed to help you process feedback with grace, build deeper trust, and foster stronger relationships. It's a must-watch for anyone looking to turn difficult conversations into opportunities for self-improvement and connection.

timestamps.topic: The value of feedback and the story of Christy
The speaker's personal flaws and the 'you're doing your thing' feedback cue
Introducing the TACT framework for receiving feedback

videos.title: The Reward of Your “Invisible” Work | LATEST Khutbah by Sh. Mohammad Elshinawy
videos.summary: This inspiring lecture explores the comforting Islamic concept that no good deed or effort is ever wasted in the eyes of Allah, even if we do not see immediate results in this life. Drawing from Quranic verses, prophetic history, and moving real-life stories from humanitarian work, the speaker reminds us to anchor our hope and devotion solely in the Divine. It's a must-watch for anyone seeking spiritual reassurance, motivation in their daily struggles, and a deeper understanding of Allah's appreciation for His creation.

timestamps.topic: The Promise of Divine Reward for the Patient and Piety-Minded
Miracles in the Field: Lessons of Faith and Perseverance in Africa
The Unwavering Justice of Allah: Your Efforts Will Never Be Denied

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
videos.summary: The future of development is shifting from manual coding to building and managing 'software factories'. This keynote provides a crucial macro view of how AI is transforming the fullstack engineering workflow, helping you position your skills ahead of the curve.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: This video offers a practical framework for developers to escape 'skill hell' and write high-quality AI engineering skills. It delivers a four-part checklist—covering triggers, structure, steering, and pruning—to help you build efficient and predictable AI agents.

timestamps.topic: The Problem of Skill Hell
User-Invoked vs. Model-Invoked Skills
Structuring and Steering AI Skills

videos.title: MIT Professor: Leetcode, P vs NP, SAT Solvers | Ryan Williams
videos.summary: Dive into the mind-bending world of computer science with Gödel Prize-winning MIT Professor Ryan Williams as he unpacks how classic algorithms can be pushed past their assumed limits. He explains why popular beliefs like the Strong Exponential Time Hypothesis might actually be false, revealing a fascinating hidden landscape of time-space tradeoffs in computation. Ultimately, this video is a masterclass in challenging conventional wisdom to uncover revolutionary, counter-intuitive shortcuts in algorithm design.

timestamps.topic: Beating Quadratic Time on the 3Sum Problem
Fine-Grained Complexity: Connecting P and NP Problems
Simulating Time with Square Root Space

videos.title: Simpan RM100 boleh jadi jutawan?
videos.summary: Building wealth starts with a solid foundation. In this highly engaging breakdown, Financial Faiz demystifies compound interest and demonstrates how minor, consistent local savings of RM100 can build substantial wealth within the Malaysian financial ecosystem.

videos.title: Why Your Nafs Owns You (And the 10-Minute Practice That Takes It Back)
videos.summary: This video reveals why standard productivity tools fail if you ignore the inner battle with your nafs (impulsive soul). It introduces muhasabah (daily self-accounting) as a simple 10-minute nightly habit to conquer bad routines and build true self-discipline.

timestamps.topic: Understanding the Nafs and the Struggle of High Performers
The Three Weapons of the Soul: Desire, Heedlessness, and Whispers
Why External Fixes Fail and the Power of 10-Minute Muhasabah

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: This video demystifies why large, do-it-all AI agents fail and introduces the shift toward 'domain-specific agents.' The core takeaway is that orchestrating networks of small, specialized AI agents using composition rather than bloated context windows is the key to creating cost-effective and highly reliable AI systems.

timestamps.topic: Defining the true nature of an AI agent
The hidden engineering hurdles of custom agents
How domain-specific agents solve the integration crisis

videos.title: The Dark Side Of Getting Rich On Youtube w/ @mattdavella
videos.summary: Discover why your standard study habits might be secretly failing you and how to unlock actual, long-term retention using brain-backed learning strategies. This video breaks down the psychological trap of the 'illusion of competence' and offers a simple, actionable shift to active recall that will save you hours of wasted effort. It is a must-watch for anyone looking to master new skills faster and study smarter, not harder.

timestamps.topic: Why rereading notes creates a false sense of mastery
How to implement active recall to double your memory retention

videos.title: EP12 #SembangKS | TTMM, PN, Calon MB, Muda, Trump dan Kulat Zombi
videos.summary: This highly engaging episode of "Sembang KS" offers a witty and sharp breakdown of Malaysia's fast-moving political landscape, focusing on the latest developments in Perikatan Nasional and MUDA. Host Zaidel Baharuddin balances political analysis with humor, making complex topics like party leadership struggles and state election strategies accessible to the everyday viewer.

timestamps.topic: Analysis of leadership changes and internal dynamics within Perikatan Nasional
Discussion on MUDA's election candidates and humorous local political commentary

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: This video offers a rare, behind-the-scenes look at how OpenGov successfully built and scaled their production-grade AI assistant, OG Assist. By sharing key architectural decisions, such as transitioning from LangGraph to a custom, type-safe 'Effect'-native framework, the presenter delivers highly actionable insights for deploying robust agents. The core takeaway is that scaling real-world AI requires robust type safety, end-to-end observability, and absolute control over the agent loop.

timestamps.topic: Introduction and Demo of OG Assist
Why OpenGov Adopted the 'Effect' Library
Rebuilding the Agent Loop for Production Scale

videos.title: Pure technical merit isn't enough
videos.summary: This video explains why great technical projects often go unnoticed without proper marketing. The speaker shares that capturing a visitor's interest in the first 10 seconds is crucial for success in the modern attention economy.

timestamps.topic: The marketing gap in technical projects
Capturing interest in the attention economy
